note: Raw GPT-5 nano per-article scores, archived when OpenAI was removed from the consensus ensemble. Excluded because the model declines to engage the framing/tone scales (scores ~0 regardless of content; pairwise r ~ 0 with all other models on those dims).

https://www.nytimes.com/2018/10/22/opini.rationale: Strong framing around political controversy and anti-trans policy shifts; limited trans voices and mostly policy/political actors drive the piece.

https://www.nytimes.com/2018/10/24/books.rationale: Strong cultural and personal narrative focus on transgender authors and works; uses quotes from trans authors and discusses genre, without advocating policy or medical care.

https://www.nytimes.com/2018/10/25/podca.rationale: Article centers political strategy; transgender angle appears as a contextual example within partisan framing rather than as subject-driven narrative.

https://www.nytimes.com/2018/10/27/opini.rationale: Strong cultural critique and personal narrative drive; advocates' framing present but not dominant; some examination of backlash and rights concerns.

https://www.nytimes.com/2018/11/27/us/tr.rationale: Strong emphasis on alleged abuse and detainee treatment; disparities between ICE and autopsy present, with quotes from Transgender Law Center and detainees shaping narrative around rights and care.
